answer.text: Government increasing spending and cutting taxes
Government reducing spending and increasing taxes to cool the economy
Government issuing more bonds in the market

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Contractionary fiscal policy = government spends less and taxes more to reduce money in circulation and cool an overheated economy. **The Trap:** Increasing spending/cutting taxes is expansionary. Cutting rates is monetary, not fiscal. Bond issuance can be part of both. **Cross-Exam Context:** Fiscal policy types tested in NISM Series XV and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Contractionary fiscal measures are unpopular politically but necessary during inflationary periods to prevent economic overheating.
